ocr.text: The following table presents a detailed analysis of the correlation between EZH2 grade and various traits over a specified
duration, specifically focusing on two critical timeframes: four years and five years.
Biomarker status  FFBF  CRFS  DMFS  PCSS  OS
4 years  P‐value  4 years  P‐value  4 years  P‐value  5 years  P‐value  5 years  P‐value
Traits/groups  S60  J60  t values
Overall  NH  ED  Clinic  P-Value*
n  %  n  %  n  %  n  %
Inappropriate use across all Infection Types  359  48.8  304  47.5  16  47.1  39  63.9  0.048
Inappropriate for Lower Respiratory Tract Infections  99  50.7  94  51.9  2  28.6  3  42.9  0.437
Inappropriate for Skin and Soft Tissue Infections  47  26.9  29  21.5  3  25.0  15  53.6  0.002
Inappropriate for Urinary Tract Infections  213  58.4  181  55.9  11  73.3  21  80.8  0.023
Each timeframe is accompanied by P-values, which serve as indicators of statistical significance in relation to the traits or
groups examined. Notably, the table highlights that for the EZH2 grade 0 category, a consistent 100% prevalence was observed
across both four-year periods, accompanied by P-values of 0.6324 and 0.1125, respectively. This suggests a lack of statistically
significant changes in the presence of this grade over time. Additionally, the columns detailing the t-values and sample sizes (n)
provide further context for the robustness of the analysis, with a sample size of 10 for each group. Overall, these findings
contribute to our understanding of the stability of EZH2 grade 0 across the selected timeframes, offering insights into the
potential implications for clinical outcomes and treatment strategies in relevant patient populations.
